examples: In the maths of supersymmetry, the Higgs emerges quite naturally.
It's helped physicists rule out certain supersymmetry models that predict otherwise.
Processes other than supersymmetry could also account for the triple lepton surplus.
Under supersymmetry, dark matter could be made up of fermions, which are superpartners of bosons.
One form of supersymmetry predicts that there should be five Higgs bosons, which are each slightly different.
